title: These Are The Skills Agencies Say Will Define 2026
text:
SUBSCRIPTION ONLY
Agencies
These Are The Skills Agencies Say Will Define 2026
As AI adoption grows, the most human skills will help talent stand out
By
Audrey Kemp
The future of brands gets decided here. Join the industry’s top marketers at Brandweek for the ideas, insights, and connections shaping what’s next.
Get your ticket.
AI is reshaping agencies at unprecedented speed, but leaders say the most important skills in 2026 and beyond will have little to do with technical mastery.
As AI adoption grows and transforms organizational needs, agencies have shed thousands of roles in 2025. Employment in advertising, PR, and related services
fell for seven straight months
up to July, and additional job cuts at the end of the year, from the 10,000 total jobs lost in Omnicom’s
acquisition of IPG
to
Dentsu’s planned 3,400
layoffs in December, leaves a flood of talent competing for a reduced number of open roles.
Audrey Kemp
Audrey Kemp is a staff reporter for Adweek based in New York City.
@audreygkemp
